title: Diverse Weather Conditions Persist Across Andhra Pradesh - The Hans India
full_text:
Andhra Pradesh is currently experiencing a range of weather patterns, with some districts recording historic high temperatures while others are witnessing unseasonal rainfall accompanied by thunder and lightning.
Prakhar Jain, Managing Director of the State Disaster Management Authority, stated that these contrasting weather conditions are expected to continue across the state for the next two days. He warned the public to stay vigilant, as some areas may face severe heatwaves, while others could experience sudden rains with lightning.
For Thursday, authorities forecast that in addition to ongoing high temperatures, severe heatwaves are likely in the Bobbili, Bondapalli, and Ramabhadrapuram mandals of Vizianagaram district; the Garugubilli, Makkuva, Pachipenta, Parvathipuram, Salur, and Seethanagaram mandals of Parvathipuram Manyam district; and the Ananthagiri mandal of Alluri district. Around 20 other mandals across the state are also expected to experience heatwaves.
Simultaneously, under a surface trough, light to moderate rains with lightning are predicted to occur sporadically in Visakhapatnam and Anakapalli districts. Similar weather is forecast in North Andhra districts and the districts of Anantapur, Sri Sathya Sai, YSR Kadapa, and Annamayya. Farmers and cattle herders have been advised to avoid standing under trees during thunderstorms.
On Wednesday, Prakhar Jain reported that the maximum temperature in Kommipadu, Nellore district, reached 44.1 degrees Celsius. He added that intense heat affected 88 mandals across 16 districts, with temperatures exceeding 41 degrees Celsius in these areas.
